metric_value: 6-12 months implementation typical for custom data mart
notes: Aberdeen finding: line-of-business managers faced 6-12 month selection/integration cycles

metric_value: Data mart in a box — pre-assembled complete package
notes: Aberdeen characterizes Oracle DMS as removing major obstacles to data mart adoption

metric_value: Departmental/line-of-business managers; 5-100 users; under $100K total cost
notes: Mass-market sweet spot defined as sub-$100K departmental deployment

metric_value: Oracle8 RDBMS included as core component of DMS
notes: DMS bundles Oracle8 database engine

metric_value: Oracle Express Server included for multidimensional analysis
notes: Express Server provides multidimensional OLAP cubing

metric_value: Oracle Discoverer included for ad-hoc query and reporting
notes: End-user ad-hoc query tool bundled

metric_value: Under $35,000 for 5 named users; scales to $50-80K for 25-50 users
notes: Aggressive pricing compared to competitive custom-build costs

metric_value: Single unified DBA console for entire suite
notes: Reduced DBA complexity vs multi-vendor integration

metric_value: Oracle Data Mart Suite includes ETL/data transformation tools
notes: ETL component handles source-system extraction and transformation

metric_value: Business Objects cited as leading standalone BI competitor
notes: Business Objects required separate purchase and integration

metric_value: Cognos Impromptu cited as leading query/reporting competitor
notes: Cognos tools required separate purchase and integration

metric_value: Red Brick cited as purpose-built data warehouse DB competitor
notes: Specialized data warehouse RDBMS focused on query performance

metric_value: Pre-packaged integrated data mart suites will dominate over custom-assembled multi-vendor solutions
notes: Aberdeen predicts Oracle's integrated approach will win mass market

metric_value: Integrated data warehouse appliances and cloud suites became dominant; Oracle, Microsoft, IBM all pursued bundled approaches; specialized standalone BI vendors (Business Objects, Cognos, Hyperion) were acquired by major platform vendors
notes: Prediction proved correct: SAP acquired Business Objects 2008; IBM acquired Cognos 2008; Oracle acquired Hyperion 2007

metric_value: NT chosen as target platform for mass-market appeal and cost-effectiveness vs Unix
notes: NT positioned against Unix alternatives for departmental deployments

metric_value: Data mart market estimated in hundreds of millions; growing rapidly
notes: Aberdeen estimated rapid growth in departmental data mart deployments

metric_value: Aberdeen rates Oracle Data Mart Suite as strong market contender
notes: Positive Aberdeen endorsement; key market validation for Oracle

metric_value: Oracle's commitment to NT platform will accelerate enterprise NT adoption for data warehousing
notes: Aberdeen predicts Oracle DMS will drive NT into data warehouse tier

metric_value: Windows NT/2000 did become dominant enterprise data warehouse platform by 2000-2003; Linux later displaced NT/Windows in data center
notes: Prediction partially correct: NT won short-term but Linux/cloud displaced Windows in data centers

metric_value: MicroStrategy referenced as high-end BI competitor focused on enterprise scale
notes: MicroStrategy remained active and public; niche enterprise BI
